footer.description: Gan Montessori Second Home — a warm, family-oriented Montessori kindergarten in Israel, nurturing children from birth to age six.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
content: Gan Montessori Second Home (“we”, “us”, or “our”) respects your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect personal information when you use our website and services.

We collect information you provide directly, such as your name, email address, phone number, ID number, your child’s name and date of birth, banking details, and any other information required for registration or communication.
We use this information to manage registrations, communicate with parents, process payments, comply with legal obligations, and improve our services.

We do not sell your personal information. We may share it with trusted service providers (e.g., hosting, email, payment processors) only when necessary and under appropriate confidentiality obligations, or when required by Israeli law.
We implement reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect your data. However, no internet transmission is completely secure.

Under the Israeli Protection of Privacy Law, 5741-1981, you have the right to access, correct, or request deletion of your personal data. To exercise these rights, contact us at gansecondhome@gmail.com.

termsOfService.title: Terms of Service
content: By accessing or using the Gan Montessori Second Home website and services, you agree to these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use the site.
You must provide accurate and complete information when registering your child or creating an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials.
Our services are provided for informational and registration purposes related to our kindergarten. We reserve the right to refuse or cancel registration at our discretion.
All content on this site, including text, images, and logos, is the property of Gan Montessori Second Home or its licensors and is protected by applicable intellectual property laws.
To the fullest extent permitted by Israeli law, we are not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the site or services.
We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.
These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Israel, and any dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts in Israel.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
content: Gan Montessori Second Home is committed to making its website and services accessible to all people, including persons with disabilities, in accordance with the Israeli Equal Rights for Persons with Disabilities Regulations (Service Accessibility Adjustments), 5773-2013, and the Israeli Standard IS 5568, based on WCAG 2.0 Level AA guidelines.

Our website includes a persistent accessibility button (bottom left) that provides immediate adjustments: font size control, high contrast mode, grayscale mode, link highlighting, and increased line spacing. Additional features include semantic HTML5 with appropriate ARIA roles; full keyboard navigation support; screen reader compatibility (ARIA labels on interactive elements); RTL (Hebrew) and LTR (English) direction support; responsive design adapted to all screen sizes; and clear focus indicators provided by Material UI.

This website complies with the requirements of Israeli Standard IS 5568 at Level AA, in accordance with the WCAG 2.0 (Web Content Accessibility Guidelines) of the W3C. We continuously work to improve the accessibility of our website and meet the latest standards.

The website was built using accessibility-supporting technologies, including semantic HTML5, React with Material UI (MUI), and modern browser standards. It is designed to work with assistive technologies such as JAWS, NVDA, VoiceOver, and TalkBack.

We invest significant effort in making all website content accessible. However, some third-party content or embedded media may not be fully accessible. We are continuously working to improve these elements.

We welcome feedback on accessibility issues or suggestions for improvement. You can contact us at: Email: gansecondhome@gmail.com; WhatsApp: +972 52-708-2519. We commit to addressing accessibility inquiries within 7 business days.

This accessibility statement was written in accordance with Israeli law requirements: Equal Rights for Persons with Disabilities Law, 5758-1998; Equal Rights for Persons with Disabilities Regulations (Service Accessibility Adjustments), 5773-2013; and Israeli Standard IS 5568 — Web Content Accessibility. The website was last reviewed and updated for accessibility in March 2026.

auth.activateTitle: Hey {{name}}, ready to activate your account?

home.montessoriBody1: The Montessori method is an educational approach developed by Dr. Maria Montessori that emphasises hands-on, child-centred learning.
home.montessoriBody2: Children learn at their own pace in carefully prepared environments that encourage independence, curiosity and respect for others.
home.montessoriBody3: Our gan offers a warm, family atmosphere where every child is supported to grow socially, emotionally and academically.
home.scheduleNote: Schedules may vary on holidays and special events. Contact us for the latest updates.
home.cardMethodDesc: Discover an educational philosophy that puts the child at the centre of their own learning journey.
home.cardAboutDesc: Our classroom is a carefully prepared space where children can explore, create and develop confidence every day.
home.cardServicesDesc: I'm Sarah Picovky and I want to invite you to meet our sweet gan, a warm place with family atmosphere and healthy home made meals.

welcome.title: Welcome to Gan Montessori Second Home.
welcome.p1: Thank you for choosing us to be part of one of the most important stages of your child's life. We understand the trust that comes with leaving your child in someone else's care, and we consider it a privilege to walk alongside your family during these early years.
welcome.p2: At Gan second home, we believe that childhood deserves to be protected, respected, and celebrated. Every child is unique, capable, and naturally curious. Our role is not simply to teach, but to carefully prepare an environment where children can explore, discover, and develop confidence at their own pace.
welcome.p3: Our classrooms are thoughtfully designed to encourage independence, concentration, creativity, and joyful learning through hands-on experiences. Every activity has a purpose, every routine provides security, and every interaction is guided by kindness, patience, and respect.
welcome.p4: We value strong partnerships with families because we believe children thrive when home and school work together. Open communication, mutual respect, and consistency create the foundation for a positive and meaningful early childhood experience.
welcome.p5: Our goal is for every child to arrive each morning feeling safe, loved, and excited to learn, and to leave each afternoon proud of what they accomplished.
welcome.p6: Thank you for becoming part of our community.
welcome.p7: We look forward to sharing this beautiful journey with your family.
welcome.missionText: To provide a warm, safe, and inspiring Montessori environment where every child is encouraged to develop independence, confidence, compassion, creativity, and a lifelong love of learning.
welcome.visionP1: We strive to create a children's house where every child feels respected, valued, and free to grow into their fullest potential.
welcome.visionP2: By nurturing curiosity, responsibility, kindness, and self-confidence, we hope to build a strong foundation that will support each child throughout life.

values.name: Respect
values.desc: We treat every child with dignity and kindness. Respect is at the heart of every interaction.

values.name: Independence
values.desc: Children are encouraged to do things for themselves, building confidence through everyday experiences.

values.name: Curiosity
values.desc: We inspire children to ask questions, explore, experiment, and discover the world around them.

values.name: Responsibility
values.desc: Children learn to care for themselves, others, and their environment.

values.name: Community
values.desc: Parents and teachers work together to create a consistent and supportive environment for every child.

values.name: Joy
values.desc: Learning should be meaningful, engaging, and full of wonder.

welcome.approachP1: At THE NEST, learning happens through exploration rather than memorization.
welcome.approachP2: Children are given the freedom to choose purposeful activities within a carefully prepared environment designed to support their natural development.
welcome.approachClosing: Rather than rushing children through childhood, we believe in giving them the time, space, and encouragement they need to become confident, capable, and compassionate individuals.

about.title: About Montessori Method
about.body1: The Montessori Method was developed over a century ago and remains one of the most respected educational approaches in the world.
about.body2: It is based on the belief that children are naturally eager to learn and capable of initiating learning in a supportive, thoughtfully prepared environment.
about.body3: In our gan, children choose activities that match their interests and developmental stage, building concentration, coordination and self-discipline.
about.body4: Mixed-age classrooms allow younger children to learn from older peers, while older children reinforce their knowledge by helping others.
about.body5: Teachers act as guides, observing each child and introducing materials at the right moment to support individual growth.

method.title: The Montessori Method: Education for the Future
method.body1: Created by Maria Montessori, the first female doctor in Italy, this method emerged from careful observation of how children naturally learn.
method.body2: Unlike traditional classrooms, Montessori environments encourage movement, choice and self-correction rather than rigid instruction.
method.body3: Materials are designed to be self-correcting, allowing children to recognise and fix their own mistakes without adult intervention.
method.body4: The approach nurtures a lifelong love of learning by respecting each child's rhythm and interests.
method.body5: At Gan Second Home, we integrate Montessori principles with a caring, home-like setting for children aged from birth to six.
